brand.name: The WOL Collective
brand.navTagline: By us. For us. Against what was built to erase us.
brand.quote: "Style as protest. Fabric as memory. Movement as design."

gate.title: Unlock the first drop
gate.intro: Built for Black voices, Black futures, and everyone committed to breaking old systems.
gate.body: Enter your email for a 10% launch code and join the drop list before release.
gate.ownerPrompt: Are you the owner of The WOL Collective? Input password here.
gate.waitlistNote: Drop code is sent when collection goes live.
gate.waitlistFallback: This GitHub Pages version stores signups in this browser only until you connect a real form endpoint.

headline: Rewrite
The
System
hero.title: One product. One message. No silence.
hero.text: The WOL Collective is an Afro-centred clothing project building garments around memory, resistance, and cultural authorship. The first drop stays intentionally focused: one statement piece, one clear voice.

manifesto.heading: A uniform for memory, resistance, and belonging.
manifesto.body: WOL is designed around the idea that clothing can carry witness. Every line, every seam, every silhouette is treated as a record of pressure, survival, and presence.

product.name: Product 001: The System Breaker Overshirt
product.description: A heavyweight black overshirt cut with calm structure, contrast seam mapping, and a clean silhouette intended to feel ceremonial without losing street function.
product.story: Built as a single-anchor release, the overshirt is meant to be worn as an everyday uniform. It balances restraint and defiance: soft drape, firm shoulders, and a message carried through proportion rather than noise.

concepts.title: Courtline Quilting
concepts.body: Stitch paths echo the legal routes communities are forced to navigate, with hidden interior map lines appearing only at close range.

concepts.title: Noiseprint Denim
concepts.body: Fabric surface patterns derived from chant rhythm and public gathering audio, converted into raised tonal textures.

concepts.title: Archive Hem
concepts.body: Each hem carries stitched date markers referencing moments of refusal, assembly, and collective care.

footer.right: Access is intentional. Culture is non-negotiable.
